avara.title: AVARA — Autonomous Validation & Agent Risk Authority
avara.markdown:
# AVARA (Autonomous Validation & Agent Risk Authority)

AVARA is a **runtime security layer** that enforces intent, permissions, provenance, and safety at execution time, not training time.

## Core Features
- **Intent Validation**: Verifies that the agent's action aligns with the user's original goal.
- **Circuit Breakers**: Automatically halts agent loops if anomalous behavior is detected.
- **Audit Ledger**: Immutable record of every decision and tool call.

## Integration
AVARA acts as a shim between the Agent Framework (LangChain/CrewAI) and the World.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    Agent->>AVARA: Intent: Pay ₹200 to Merchant X
    AVARA->>CAPS: Check Policy
    CAPS-->>AVARA: Approved
    AVARA->>Agent: Proceed
```

See also: [[CAPS]] for the payment implementation.
avara.html: # AVARA (Autonomous Validation & Agent Risk Authority) AVARA is a **runtime security layer** that enforces intent, permissions, provenance, and safety at execution time, not training time. ## Core Features - **Intent Validation**: Verifies that the agent's action aligns with the user's original goal. - **Circuit Breakers**: Automatically halts agent loops if anomalous behavior is detected. - **Audit Ledger**: Immutable record of every decision and tool call. ## Integration AVARA acts as a shim between the Agent Framework (LangChain/CrewAI) and the World. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram Agent->>AVARA: Intent: Pay ₹200 to Merchant X AVARA->>CAPS: Check Policy CAPS-->>AVARA: Approved AVARA->>Agent: Proceed ``` See also: CAPS for the payment implementation.

caps.title: CAPS — Context-Aware Agentic Payment System
caps.markdown:
# CAPS (Context-Aware Agentic Payment System)

CAPS is a **deterministic payment kernel** designed for autonomous agents. It decouples reasoning (LLMs) from execution (UPI Lite) using policy-based safety gates.

## The Problem
AI agents in 2026 are increasingly autonomous but lack a safe way to handle money. Giving an LLM direct access to a bank account is a catastrophic security risk.

## The Solution
CAPS treats the LLM as an **untrusted intent source**. 
1. The LLM expresses a desire to pay.
2. The CAPS **Policy Engine** intercepts the request.
3. The request is validated against deterministic rules (e.g., max ₹500/txn, merchant reputation).
4. Execution happens via **UPI Lite Google ADK**.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[User Intent] --> B[LLM Agent]
    B --> C{CAPS Policy Engine}
    C -- Valid --> D[UPI Lite Execution]
    C -- Invalid --> E[Rejection / Human in loop]
    D --> F[Merchant VPA]
```

## Tech Stack
- **Reasoning**: Ollama (Local) / Gemini
- **Safety**: Python-based Deterministic Guardrails
- **Payment**: UPI Lite / Google Pay ADK
- **Monitoring**: Real-time Fraud Intelligence Loop

[[AVARA]] provides the runtime governance for the agents using CAPS.
caps.html: # CAPS (Context-Aware Agentic Payment System) CAPS is a **deterministic payment kernel** designed for autonomous agents. It decouples reasoning (LLMs) from execution (UPI Lite) using policy-based safety gates. ## The Problem AI agents in 2026 are increasingly autonomous but lack a safe way to handle money. Giving an LLM direct access to a bank account is a catastrophic security risk. ## The Solution CAPS treats the LLM as an **untrusted intent source**. 1. The LLM expresses a desire to pay. 2. The CAPS **Policy Engine** intercepts the request. 3. The request is validated against deterministic rules (e.g., max ₹500/txn, merchant reputation). 4. Execution happens via **UPI Lite Google ADK**. ```mermaid graph TD A[User Intent] --> B[LLM Agent] B --> C{CAPS Policy Engine} C -- Valid --> D[UPI Lite Execution] C -- Invalid --> E[Rejection / Human in loop] D --> F[Merchant VPA] ``` ## Tech Stack - **Reasoning**: Ollama (Local) / Gemini - **Safety**: Python-based Deterministic Guardrails - **Payment**: UPI Lite / Google Pay ADK - **Monitoring**: Real-time Fraud Intelligence Loop AVARA provides the runtime governance for the agents using CAPS.

floework.title: Floework — Real-time Behavioral Telemetry
floework.markdown:
# Floework (Real-time Behavioral Telemetry)

Floework is a distributed tracing system designed to capture and analyze **human interaction sequences** in real-time.

## The Objective
Standard APM (Application Performance Monitoring) tracks CPU and Memory. Floework tracks **Flow**. 
- Where does the user hesitate?
- Which interaction sequence leads to a "Predictive Hang"?

## Integration
Floework hooks into the UI event loop and streams high-fidelity telemetry to a Go-based ingestion engine.

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Client UI] -- Protocol Buffers --> B[Ingestion Engine]
    B --> C[Stream Processor]
    C --> D[K-PHD Analysis]
    D --> E[Real-time Feedback]
```

Related: [[K-PHD]] uses Floework signals to predict kernel-level stalls.
floework.html: # Floework (Real-time Behavioral Telemetry) Floework is a distributed tracing system designed to capture and analyze **human interaction sequences** in real-time. ## The Objective Standard APM (Application Performance Monitoring) tracks CPU and Memory. Floework tracks **Flow**. - Where does the user hesitate? - Which interaction sequence leads to a "Predictive Hang"? ## Integration Floework hooks into the UI event loop and streams high-fidelity telemetry to a Go-based ingestion engine. ```mermaid graph LR A[Client UI] -- Protocol Buffers --> B[Ingestion Engine] B --> C[Stream Processor] C --> D[K-PHD Analysis] D --> E[Real-time Feedback] ``` Related: K-PHD uses Floework signals to predict kernel-level stalls.

k-phd.title: K-PHD — Kernel-level Predictive Hang Detector
k-phd.markdown:
# K-PHD (Kernel-level Predictive Hang Detector)

K-PHD is a Linux kernel module that embeds into the scheduler hot-path to measure nanosecond-level process wait times.

## Mechanics
By hooking into `sched_switch` and `sched_wakeup` tracepoints, K-PHD computes an Exponential Moving Average (EMA) of wait latencies for every PID.

## Prediction
If the EMA exceeds a threshold (e.g., 5ms), K-PHD signals a **Predictive Hang** via Netlink to userspace before the application actually freezes.

```c
// Example tracepoint hook
TRACE_EVENT(sched_switch,
    TP_PROTO(bool preempt, struct task_struct *prev, struct task_struct *next),
    ...
);
```

Related: [[Floework]] uses similar behavioral signals but at the application layer.
k-phd.html: # K-PHD (Kernel-level Predictive Hang Detector) K-PHD is a Linux kernel module that embeds into the scheduler hot-path to measure nanosecond-level process wait times. ## Mechanics By hooking into `sched_switch` and `sched_wakeup` tracepoints, K-PHD computes an Exponential Moving Average (EMA) of wait latencies for every PID. ## Prediction If the EMA exceeds a threshold (e.g., 5ms), K-PHD signals a **Predictive Hang** via Netlink to userspace before the application actually freezes. ```c // Example tracepoint hook TRACE_EVENT(sched_switch, TP_PROTO(bool preempt, struct task_struct *prev, struct task_struct *next), ... ); ``` Related: Floework uses similar behavioral signals but at the application layer.

shield.title: SHIELD — Adaptive Fraud Detection
shield.markdown:
# SHIELD (Adaptive Fraud Detection)

SHIELD is a behavioral biometrics engine that identifies users based on how they interact with their devices, rather than what they know or have.

## Key Vectors
- **Touch Dynamics**: Pressure, area, and curvature of swipes.
- **Sensor Fusion**: Accelerometer and Gyroscope patterns during interaction.
- **Sequence Analysis**: The order and timing of menu navigation.

## Performance
SHIELD achieves a **99.2% Accuracy** in identifying unauthorized session takeovers by analyzing the first 5 seconds of interaction.

```mermaid
pie title Feature Importance
    "Touch Dynamics" : 45
    "Sensor Fusion" : 30
    "Navigation Sequence" : 25
```

See also: [[CAPS]] uses SHIELD scores to authorize high-value transactions.
shield.html: # SHIELD (Adaptive Fraud Detection) SHIELD is a behavioral biometrics engine that identifies users based on how they interact with their devices, rather than what they know or have. ## Key Vectors - **Touch Dynamics**: Pressure, area, and curvature of swipes. - **Sensor Fusion**: Accelerometer and Gyroscope patterns during interaction. - **Sequence Analysis**: The order and timing of menu navigation. ## Performance SHIELD achieves a **99.2% Accuracy** in identifying unauthorized session takeovers by analyzing the first 5 seconds of interaction. ```mermaid pie title Feature Importance "Touch Dynamics" : 45 "Sensor Fusion" : 30 "Navigation Sequence" : 25 ``` See also: CAPS uses SHIELD scores to authorize high-value transactions.
